title: Chloroform Phenol Phage Genome Isolation
author: Marijn Ceelen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.7kvhkw6

steps.title: Use cleaned phage stock from the phage stock protocol
steps.instruction: Use cleaned phage stock from the phage stock protocol. Add 50 µL DNase I 10x buffer, 1 µL DNase I (1 U/µL), and 1 µL RNase A (10 mg/mL) for 01:30:00 at 37 °C without shaking to remove E. coli DNA and RNA.

steps.title: Add 20 µL of 0
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of 0.5 Molarity (M) EDTA (final concentration 20 Molarity (M) ) and incubate for 00:15:00 at 75 °C to deactivate DNase I and RNase A

steps.title: Add 2 µL Proteinase K (20...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL Proteinase K (20 mg/mL) and 50 µL SDS 10% to digest the phage capsid and incubate overnight at 56 °C without shaking.

steps.title: Add equal volume phenol/chloroform/iosamyl alcohol (25:24:1)...
steps.instruction: Add equal volume phenol/chloroform/iosamyl alcohol (25:24:1) and mix well (do not vortex, as genome is easily damaged)

steps.title: Centrifuge at room temperature for 00:10:00 at 10000 x g
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at room temperature for 00:10:00 at 10000 x g

steps.title: Add equal volume chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (24:1)...
steps.instruction: Add equal volume chloroform/isoamyl alcohol (24:1) and mix well (do not vortex, as genome is easily damaged)

steps.title: Centrifuge at room temperature for 00:10:00 at 10000 x g
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at room temperature for 00:10:00 at 10000 x g

steps.title: Add 1/10 volume of Sodium acetate 3 Molarity (M)
steps.instruction: Add 1/10 volume of Sodium acetate 3 Molarity (M)

steps.title: Dry pellet
steps.instruction: Dry pellet. Heating to 50 °C c increases drying.
